title: Sample Project: Ingestion Pipeline
summary: A Databricks ingestion pipeline is used as a sample project to show how Superpowers turns vague coding-agent requests into governed spec-driven delivery.
businessGoal: Demonstrate the Superpowers artifact trail and quality gates that move an ingestion pipeline from clarified intent to approved spec, planned work, test-first implementation, review evidence, and verified completion.

components.name: Data Lakehouse
components.role: Illustrates the target architecture that Superpowers brainstorming and specification must make explicit before a coding agent starts implementation.

components.name: Delta Lake
components.role: Illustrates spec-driven data contracts, quality gates, schema evolution decisions, and regression evidence for bronze, silver, and gold pipeline behavior.

components.name: Unity Catalog
components.role: Illustrates governance requirements that Superpowers artifacts capture as access policies, lineage expectations, review findings, and verification checks.

components.name: Databricks SQL
components.role: Illustrates stakeholder-facing acceptance criteria where Superpowers verification proves dashboards reconcile to governed pipeline outputs.

components.name: Lakebase
components.role: Illustrates serving-boundary decisions where Superpowers planning requires explicit freshness, synchronization, stale-state, and failure-mode behavior.

dataFlow: Receipt JSON and PDF-derived text land in bronze Delta Lake tables so the sample ingestion pipeline has a concrete source contract for Superpowers specification work.
Silver pipelines standardize store identifiers, timestamps, tax fields, SKU references, discounts, and tender metadata while Superpowers quality gates require malformed receipts to be quarantined.
Unity Catalog classifies customer identifiers and payment-adjacent fields, then the Superpowers review stage validates access boundaries before analyst rollout.
Gold marts summarize daily sales, return rates, tax variance, and promotion performance as the Databricks SQL example used for acceptance checks.
A curated receipt service table is synchronized into Lakebase so the sample package can verify operational lookup freshness and stale-state behavior.
successMetrics: Superpowers artifacts trace each implementation task back to an approved spec decision and a verification check.
Receipt freshness is under 30 minutes for analytics and under five minutes for Lakebase support lookup extracts.
At least 99.5 percent of valid receipt events reach silver tables without manual intervention.
Databricks SQL finance dashboards reconcile gross sales, taxes, returns, and discounts to point-of-sale control totals within 0.2 percent.
Unity Catalog lineage shows source-to-dashboard traceability for each gold KPI table.
risks: Vague coding-agent prompts can skip the spec and produce a Databricks build that works locally but lacks governed acceptance evidence.
Schema drift from point-of-sale firmware updates can break receipt parsing unless Delta Lake expectations and quarantine paths are enforced.
PII leakage is possible if Unity Catalog tags and grants are not reviewed before analyst access is opened.
Lakebase serving tables can diverge from lakehouse truth unless synchronization freshness and failure behavior are specified.

phases.label: Brainstorm / Design
phases.focus: Clarify users, goals, assumptions, risks, and success criteria for the ingestion pipeline sample before Databricks design choices.

phases.label: Specification
phases.focus: Define receipt schemas, quality thresholds, lineage, access rules, SLA targets, and dashboard acceptance checks.

phases.label: Workspace Isolation
phases.focus: Create an isolated Git workspace so the sample remains scoped, reviewable, and safe to abandon.

phases.label: Implementation Planning
phases.focus: Break the approved specification into ordered, testable work for Delta Lake, Unity Catalog, Databricks SQL, and Lakebase.

phases.label: Execution
phases.focus: Implement the planned sample increments while preserving links between code, spec decisions, tests, and review evidence.

phases.label: Test-Driven Development
phases.focus: Write failing tests for quality gates, governance boundaries, dashboard reconciliation, and Lakebase freshness before implementation.

phases.label: Systematic Debugging
phases.focus: Diagnose failures through observed evidence, focused hypotheses, and root-cause experiments instead of Databricks guesswork.

phases.label: Code Review
phases.focus: Review changes against the specification, tests, maintainability expectations, and residual sample-project risks.

phases.label: Verification
phases.focus: Verify freshness, quarantine rate, reconciliation accuracy, lineage, and serving sync behavior before completion claims.

phases.label: Branch Completion
phases.focus: Package the branch with verification evidence, review disposition, changed files, and merge or pull-request readiness.
